title: pusaetha polyphylla
side_effects: Pusaetha polyphylla can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, due to its toxic compounds. Overconsumption of its leaves or flowers can also lead to allergic reactions, such as skin rashes and respiratory problems, in some individuals. Furthermore, improper use of the plant can cause cardiovascular issues, including increased heart rate and blood pressure, which may be hazardous for people with pre-existing heart conditions. In severe cases, ingestion of the plant's toxic compounds can lead to organ damage and even death.
